And this is so interesting because I think this is something baked into the post-training where it just wants to keep adding more things. Oh, there's a bug. Let me add a patch to fix it. You don't like this? Let me like make this other thing more blah blah blah. And I'm like, no, the process is really about editing down the things you don't want. But the model doesn't seem to want to have an opinion on what to edit down. And when people talk online about taste, taste, taste, taste, taste. All taste is, I think, is editing from with a point of view.
turns.zh: 有一件事我不知道你是否注意到了，尤其是 Claude 最新的 Sonnet 模型，当你想要做某事时，尤其是当你给出批评并想要改变某事时，AI 模型想要添加。它们想要添加或打补丁作为改进的方式。但我认为设计过程实际上是关于删减。这很有趣，因为我认为这是后训练中固有的东西，它只想不断添加更多东西。哦，有个 bug，让我加个补丁修复它。你不喜欢这个？让我让其他东西更怎样怎样。而我说，不，过程实际上是关于删减你不想要的东西。但模型似乎不想对删减什么有自己的看法。当人们在网上谈论品味、品味、品味时，我认为所有品味都是从某个角度进行删减。

turns.en: Oo, I like that.
turns.zh: 哦，我喜欢这个说法。

turns.en: Right. Taste is just saying no. Taste is just judgment. It's going like I like this, I don't like this. And if you liked everything, then you have no taste because having taste means you don't like some things. And you have to just say no to a lot of things. What Johnny and the Dieter Rams school of industrial design, the idea is you create a coherent form that contains the truth and nothing but the truth. It's a distillation. But I think often people mistake the depth of thought needed to create something that coheres for let's just make something that looks aesthetically simple.
turns.zh: 没错。品味就是说“不”。品味就是判断。它就像“我喜欢这个，我不喜欢这个”。如果你什么都喜欢，那你就没有品味，因为拥有品味意味着你不喜欢某些东西。你必须对很多事情说“不”。Johnny 和 Dieter Rams 的工业设计学派，其理念是创造一个连贯的形式，包含真理，且只有真理。这是一种提炼。但我认为人们常常把创造连贯事物所需的深度思考误解为“让我们只做看起来美学上简单的东西”。

turns.en: That is almost never the case of anything great. The cost of creating coherent work has gone up though because now it is so fast and easy to make incoherent. That's what we call slop — incoherence, basically. Even in the image making we do now, I miss some of the more raw outputs from the models three or four years ago. For a brief window of time, I felt like I was seeing how computers perceive the world in a way that was native to computers. And then we've post-trained all of the weirdness away, and now it just makes big titty goth girls because that's what humans think they want. I feel like we've lost a window into the machine consciousness, and I feel a bit sad about that.
turns.zh: 伟大的事物几乎从来不是这样。创作连贯作品的成本反而上升了，因为现在要制造不连贯的东西实在太快太容易了。这就是我们所说的“垃圾”——基本上就是不连贯。即使在我们现在的图像创作中，我也怀念三四年前模型那些更原始的输出。有那么短暂的一段时间，我感觉自己看到了计算机以它自己的方式感知世界。然后我们通过后训练把所有的怪异都磨平了，现在它只会生成大胸哥特女孩，因为人类觉得这就是他们想要的。我觉得我们失去了一扇通往机器意识的窗户，对此我感到有些遗憾。

turns.en: Incoherence is the lack of a point of view in many ways too. Slop can be beautiful, but if it doesn't have the human truth or a deeper why, then what are we looking at?
turns.zh: 不连贯在很多方面也意味着缺乏观点。垃圾可能看起来很漂亮，但如果它没有人性的真实或更深层的意义，那我们到底在看什么呢？

turns.en: All of our work — even we've created it with AI models — but I think it feels deeply human because we spent so long editing things from our point of view.
turns.zh: 我们所有的作品——即使是用 AI 模型创作的——但我觉得它们之所以感觉非常人性化，是因为我们花了很长时间从自己的视角去编辑。

turns.en: Which is why it's so unique too. I had a question I was going to ask you: what was the north star for the brand? What were you working towards? And I love the answer that you already gave, which is because it was so unique. How did you get there?
turns.zh: 这也是它如此独特的原因。我本来想问你的问题是：这个品牌的北极星是什么？你在朝着什么方向努力？我很喜欢你刚才的回答，因为正是这种独特性。你是怎么做到的？

transcript.sec: On design and the human experience

turns.en: What will make you feel something? What makes me feel like what is my stomach pointing towards? I find it really interesting that in so many sectors of design, especially high culture and high design, people seem to have contempt towards the human experience. There's a certain genre of design or products where they're like, 'Don't scroll slop anymore. Go with the screenless device. That is how you're supposed to live your life.' But there's something really human about us wanting to look at things that we find appealing. The human experience isn't just going on long walks along the Parisian canal and sipping a latte. The human experience has lots of edges to it. It has envy, wrath, sex, lust, jealousy, and violence. That is also a part of the human experience. Should we really take all of that away? Is that still honest to who we are? I don't know that we need to create things that reward our vices necessarily. When we design things, even from the point of rewarding our virtues, I don't think we should shame humans for having vices. That's simply who we are. I think there's something about design, especially high design now, that feels utterly sexless. There's no desire. There's no lust. We've sanded away anything that could potentially be arousing, but also we've sanded away anything that feels objectionable. In doing so, we've created essentially nothing. And to me, that is the ultimate slop. I love having patina on my iPhone where I drop it and it shows its age. I love having wrinkles that show you that someone has lifted their eyebrows a lot.
turns.zh: 什么会让你有感觉？什么让我觉得我的直觉在指向哪里？我觉得很有趣的是，在设计领域的很多方面，尤其是高端文化和高端设计，人们似乎对人类体验抱有轻蔑。有一种设计或产品流派会说：“别再刷垃圾了。用无屏设备吧。这才是你应该过的生活。”但人类想要看那些吸引我们的东西，这本身就很人性化。人类体验不仅仅是沿着巴黎运河散步、啜饮拿铁。人类体验有很多棱角：有嫉妒、愤怒、性、欲望、嫉妒和暴力。那也是人类体验的一部分。我们真的应该把这些都抹去吗？那还是对我们自己诚实吗？我不确定我们是否需要创造奖励我们恶习的东西。但当我们设计东西时，即使是从奖励美德的角度出发，我认为我们也不应该因为人类有恶习而羞辱他们。这就是我们本来的样子。我认为现在的设计，尤其是高端设计，感觉完全没有性别色彩。没有欲望，没有情欲。我们磨平了任何可能激起欲望的东西，也磨平了任何可能令人反感的东西。这样做，我们基本上创造出了虚无。对我来说，这才是终极的垃圾。我喜欢我的 iPhone 上有使用痕迹，摔了之后会显露出岁月的痕迹。我喜欢皱纹，那表明一个人经常扬起眉毛。

turns.en: Can we talk about what's next for Hivemind then? You laid this very interesting foundation, and you can imagine almost any number of stories in terms of where this could go. You hinted that even since launch, some of that thinking has evolved and new ideas have been sparked. So when you think about where this is headed, what's on your mind?
turns.zh: 那我们能谈谈 Hivemind 接下来要做什么吗？你打下了非常有趣的基础，可以想象出很多种发展方向。你暗示过，即使发布之后，一些想法也在演变，新想法被激发出来。那么，当你思考它的未来方向时，你在想什么？

turns.en: The ultimate north star of Hivemind is to give humanity access to a point of view that is honest about what people really think about things. Google has organized all the public posts and information in the world, and our algorithms and timelines feed it to us. We live in an age of abundance of content, where you can just generate infinite content. What we hunger for, I believe, is honest and truth signal. We hunger to live in shared narratives with others. When you see some of the things we're putting together, I think it will tell its own story and you'll understand. But the film is really the first act in a sequence that I refer to as 'sleight of hand.' I think people think it's a certain thing. Very soon, I think they will actually see the full ambition behind all of it, and that will be very interesting.
turns.zh: Hivemind 的终极北极星是让人类获得一个诚实的视角，了解人们真正在想什么。谷歌整理了世界上所有的公开帖子和信息，我们的算法和时间线把它们推送给我们。我们生活在一个内容丰富的时代，可以无限生成内容。我相信，我们渴望的是诚实和真实的信号。我们渴望与他人生活在共享的叙事中。当你看到我们正在整合的一些东西时，我认为它会讲述自己的故事，你会明白的。但这部电影实际上是我称之为“障眼法”的一系列动作中的第一幕。我认为人们以为它是某种东西。很快，我认为他们会真正看到这一切背后的全部野心，那将会非常有趣。
